title: Poa poidium
intro: Poa poidium, also known as Alpine Meadow-grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Poa poidium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and digestive issues, as well as to promote general well-being.
taxonomy: Poa poidium is a species within the genus Poa, which is classified under the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. The family Poaceae is further categorized under the clade commelinids, which is part of the monocot clade. Within the family Poaceae, Poa poidium is grouped under the subfamily Pooideae. The subfamily Pooideae is characterized by its grasses with two-awned spikelets and a three-veined leaf blade. Poa poidium's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other grasses.
